svg.text: 12.16 · The consistent-enough payments platform — money never lost/duplicated
strong+atomic where money moves · eventual+idempotent+reconciled everywhere else · per-operation (12.15)
THE MONEY PATH — strong + atomic
common transfer (co-located, M11/11.9): ONE single-shard ACID txn
debit+credit+ledger+idempotency-key+outbox-row (atomic, M07–M09)
cross-shard minority → SAGA (12.8) + idempotency (12.9)
local txns + compensations + durable state · never 2PC/XA
reads: authorize → primary (strong) · own history → read-your-writes (M10/10.6)
PROPAGATION — eventual + idempotent
OUTBOX event (atomic with state, 12.11) — never lost
→ no dual-write (12.10)
CDC binlog → Kafka (12.12) → idempotent consumers (12.13)
= exactly-once EFFECT
→ fraud · notifications · search · reporting warehouse
reporting/display → async replicas (eventual, M10/10.5)
substrate: each shard (M11) is itself REPLICATED (M10)
semi-sync (node-loss durable, M10/10.4/9.16) · primary reads (strong) · async replicas (eventual reporting)
CP under partition for money ops (group replication quorum + fencing, M10/10.11) · single-node ACID within (M07–M09)
THE BACKSTOP — reconciliation (12.14, M02/2.17)
independently re-derive balances from the immutable ledger · match external processor records · daily
→ detects/repairs ANY residual drift (a half-completed Saga, a missed event, a bug) — the money-never-lies safety net
money-never-lies across the WHOLE distribution: a payment is
ATOMIC (single-shard ACID / compensated Saga) · DURABLE beyond node loss (semi-sync) · never DOUBLE-applied (idempotency)
· never LOST in propagation (outbox+CDC) · exactly-once in EFFECT (idempotent consumers) · independently VERIFIED (reconciliation)
